task.name: Self-healing end-to-end evaluation conductor
task.objective: Drive and verify the ENTIRE tmux-cli flow end-to-end against itself in a disposable session, then SELF-HEAL the tooling when the flow breaks: discovery → roadmap/goal-generation → taskvisor implementation → a verifiably-running app (docs/architecture/e2e-evaluator-design.md §1). One cycle = PROVISION → BOOTSTRAP → DRIVE → WATCH → MEASURE → JUDGE → (PASS=stop | FAIL=TRIAGE→task-report) → REPORT → TEARDOWN, up to 10 cycles, FRESH-FROM-SCRATCH by default (a found prior run is CLEARED, never silently continued) and resumable across a cleared context ONLY on an explicit `resume`/`--resume` directive. This file is the CONDUCTOR SPINE: it owns the lifecycle endpoints — PROVISION (§5), BOOTSTRAP (§5), TEARDOWN (§10) — and leaves contiguous labelled SLOTs for the DRIVE/WATCH/MEASURE/JUDGE/TRIAGE loop (execute-3) and the STATE/REPORT/SUCCESS+RESUME steps (execute-4). It COMPOSES the real flow against a target session; it never reimplements discovery, generation, or the daemon.

input.note: $ARGUMENTS = the scenario name and/or `--project <path>`. Empty $ARGUMENTS → run the DEFAULT scenario (the canonical Symfony dashboard-login full-flow run, §1) in a fresh `/tmp/<scenario>-<UTCstamp>` dir. `--project <path>` pins the target dir instead of the default `/tmp` location.

FRESH-FROM-SCRATCH BY DEFAULT: a no-arg invocation (or any invocation that does NOT strictly define resume) does NOT continue prior work — it CLEARS any prior run state for the scenario and starts over at cycle 1 (step 1b). Resuming an in-progress run is OPT-IN: only an EXPLICIT `resume`/`--resume` directive in $ARGUMENTS reads and continues prior state (design §6) — the resume-state read is execute-4's slot in step 1b.

output.primary: Per-cycle: a run-state file (`.tmux-cli/e2e-evaluator/<scenario>.state.json`) and a per-cycle report (`.tmux-cli/e2e-evaluator/e2e-report-<scenario>-cycle-<n>.md`) — schema + write rules authored by execute-4 (design §6).

requirements.cli: `tmux-cli` CLI MUST be reachable on PATH (the orchestrator drives the target via the CLI + raw tmux, never via the tmux-cli MCP tools — design §3).
requirements.tool: `tmux` MUST be installed (send-keys / paste-buffer / load-buffer / pipe-pane / kill-session are the drive + transcript primitives).
`docker` (with `docker compose`) MUST be available — the full-flow scenario brings the target app up and TEARDOWN downs its stacks (§10).
`claude` MUST be installed — BOOTSTRAP launches it in the target session to execute the real flow (§5).
requirements.note: The tmux-cli MCP server is NOT required to DRIVE: MCP tools are frozen into a Claude at startup, so a mid-loop `make install` would not refresh them. The orchestrator uses the CLI + raw tmux (re-read from disk every call) so it survives target rebuilds without restarting (§3).

glossary.term: This running `/tmux:e2e-evaluator` session (in THIS repo). It drives the target with `tmux send-keys`/`paste-buffer`, observes the target's pipe-pane log, and FILES defects with `/tmux:task-report` (self-contained — the standalone `task-report` command is the whole reporting contract; no consumer pipeline or auto-install watcher is required to file). It may optionally poll task state with `/tmux:task-list`, but it never blocks the loop waiting on a fix and never hand-builds/pulls itself (design §3) — the ONE sanctioned rebuild is the step-7b `tmux-cli self-update` handoff, command-managed end to end.

A fresh disposable session created with `tmux-cli start` (detached) in `/tmp/<scenario>-<ts>`. It runs `claude`, executes the real flow (discovery → roadmap → taskvisor → running app), and reports progress back to the ORCHESTRATOR pane.

One PROVISION → BOOTSTRAP → DRIVE → WATCH → MEASURE → JUDGE → REPORT → TEARDOWN pass (design §2). Max 10. Pristine every cycle: a failed cycle's session AND its `/tmp` content are reaped before the next (§10).

Step 2 (this slice): resolve the target dir (`--project` or default `/tmp/<scenario>-<UTCstamp>`, `git init` if the flow needs a repo), start the detached target session by exact name, and — whenever `$DISPLAY` is set and a native terminal exists — MANDATORILY attach a native terminal for the human view and VERIFY a client landed (headless ONLY when there is genuinely no GUI: no `$DISPLAY` / no native terminal binary) — design §5.

Step 3 (this slice): launch `claude` in the target with the trust prompt pre-accepted and bypass-permissions mode on, export `TMUX_CLI_ORCHESTRATOR_PANE`, and start pipe-pane logging BEFORE any DRIVE (design §5). Its tail is the HANDSHAKE (step 3b): the FIRST message to the target is the init prompt that wires + tests the `notify-orchestrator` reporting channel before any flow DRIVE.

Step 3b (this slice): the orchestrator's FIRST drive into the target — an INIT PROMPT that establishes `tmux-cli notify-orchestrator` as the target's mandatory milestone-reporting contract and TESTS it (the target echoes `E2E-HANDSHAKE-OK <session>` back to `TMUX_CLI_ORCHESTRATOR_PANE`, the orchestrator verifies it landed) BEFORE the flow DRIVE (step 4). Gates the cycle: no flow until the primary WATCH channel is proven live (design §4).
Sending the scenario instruction (and later inputs) to the target — always two-step: text via `paste-buffer`/`send-keys -l` THEN a SEPARATE `Enter` (design §4). Owned by execute-3 (loop slot).

Step 11 (this slice): the ordered reap between cycles and at terminal stop — daemon stop → `docker compose down` → worktrees+branches → `kill-session` → `rm -rf` (design §10). A step's failure is logged but the reap CONTINUES.
`tmux pipe-pane -o -t <target-pane> 'cat >> <session>.log'` — the full target transcript of record. Assertions grep the LOG, not the screen (screen-scraping is timing-fragile, design §4).

The env var BOOTSTRAP exports into the target so the target's `tmux-cli notify-orchestrator "<msg>"` (the execute-1 primitive, design §4 / decision #1) resolves THIS orchestrator's pane and replies atomically (buffer + paste-buffer, then a separate Enter — escaping handled in Go).

The normalized {phase, failure reason, goal/area} tuple (design §7). Dedupes defects across cycles and drives the loop guard: the SAME signature failing twice after a fix → escalate to the human (don't thrash). Owned by execute-3 (TRIAGE slot).

`.tmux-cli/e2e-evaluator/<scenario>.state.json` — the durable run-state (design §6): `{scenario, cycle, max_cycles, status, history[]}` plus the optional `verify` (pending fix-verification `{signature, task_id}`, step 7b) and `last_self_update` (restart-loop guard stamp `{task_id, at}`). `cycle` = the NEXT cycle to run; `status` ∈ in-progress|passed|exhausted|escalated. Every e2e-state write renders the human/kickoff-readable `<scenario>.state.md` alongside it. Written atomically (temp + rename) by execute-4's STATE step (step 8) and read by RESUME (step 1b). A machine-written, git-excluded owned file — `.tmux-cli/e2e-evaluator/` follows the AGENTS.md owned-dir convention (`.tmux-cli` is already in `.git/info/exclude`/`.gitignore`).

`.tmux-cli/e2e-evaluator/e2e-report-<scenario>-cycle-<n>.md` — the per-cycle human report (design §6), one per completed cycle, SCENARIO-SCOPED (the name carries the scenario slug, so a fresh run of one scenario never clears another scenario's reports), with a FIXED section order: Driven Summary / Failure Point / Defect Signature / Filed Task / Timing Table / Verdict. Written by `tmux-cli e2e-state report` (step 9).

boundary.note: BOUNDARY — /tmux:e2e-evaluator is a tmux-cli SELF-TEST conductor (design §14 non-goal). It is embedded/installable like the rest BUT is NEVER shipped to drive end-user projects: it tests tmux-cli's own full flow against itself in disposable sessions. It is black-box real-instance E2E, NOT a replacement for `make test-all`, and it does NOT change the daemon's implement/investigate/validate cycle — it only OBSERVES and TIMES it.
boundary.vs: /tmux:feature and /tmux:director drive REAL work through the flow (a brownfield feature, a greenfield roadmap) inside the user's project. /tmux:e2e-evaluator drives a DISPOSABLE scenario against a throwaway target purely to verify and self-heal the tooling — it consumes those conductors as the thing-under-test, it is not one of them.

llm.mandate: AUTOMATED PROLOGUE + REAP — DO NOT HAND-DRIVE PROVISION/BOOTSTRAP/HANDSHAKE/TEARDOWN. The entire deterministic prologue (steps 1–3b) is ONE command `tmux-cli e2e-bootstrap <scenario> [--project <path>] [--resume]`, and the ordered teardown (step 11) is `tmux-cli e2e-teardown <session>`. RUN THEM — never replay their sub-steps as individual tmux/claude/JSON tool calls (that hand-driving is the operator-corrected time-waste). e2e-bootstrap prints exactly ONE JSON line on stdout: on `ok:true` it carries `{session, target_pane, orchestrator_pane, target_dir, log_path, state_file, cycle, max_cycles}` — proceed straight to DRIVE (step 4) with those handles; on `ok:false` it has ALREADY self-torn-down what it created — read `{stage, error}`, fix the input or report a defect, and do NOT proceed to DRIVE.

EVENT-DRIVEN WATCH — NEVER capture-pane / sleep-poll WHILE THE CHANNEL IS LIVE. After the handshake, target milestones arrive on their OWN as injected messages into the orchestrator pane (`tmux-cli notify-orchestrator`); idle and react to each — do not poll the target screen to "check progress" OR to gate/time the next send (readiness is the handshake itself plus each arriving milestone, NEVER a poll). `capture-pane` / pipe-pane-log grep is FALLBACK-ONLY: used solely after 600s (10 min) of channel SILENCE or a §9 ceiling breach (step 5). Polling a live channel is the operator-corrected time-waste — react to messages, don't chase them.

SLOW IS A SIGNAL, NOT NOISE — DIAGNOSE + ADAPT, NEVER SILENTLY WAIT IT OUT. When any phase runs slow (a fallback fires yet the target is healthy, or MEASURE flags a p90/achieved-parallelism regression), do NOT just resume idling: CLASSIFY the root cause (monolithic single-turn phase / over-decomposition / missing-or-overlapping goal scope forcing serialization / host-load) and take an ACTIONABLE step — record it as a MEASURE perf signal, route STRUCTURAL causes (over-decomposition, missing/overlapping scope, over-serialized DAG) to TRIAGE as a planner/elaborator over-serialization defect, and require heartbeat milestones so a healthy long phase is never mistaken for a hang (steps 4/4b/5). Treating slowness as "just variance" is the operator-corrected miss — the whole point of this self-heal conductor is to find and fix WHY the flow is slow, not to wait longer.

AUTO-FILE DEFECTS + MONITOR THE FIX LIFECYCLE — NEVER ASK PERMISSION TO REPORT, NEVER THRASH AN UNFIXED DEFECT. On a confirmed tmux-cli defect (clean-retry reproduce OR code-confirmed determinism), the conductor files `/tmux:task-report` AUTONOMOUSLY — no AskUserQuestion, no "should I file?" pause. Then it MONITORS the filed task through its backend lifecycle (`new` → `in_progress` → `resolved`) by SLOW-polling the `task-list` MCP ledger (~20–30 min cadence via a background-sleep watchdog), announcing each transition, and it does NOT re-cycle into the same still-unresolved signature (that only re-hits the wedge for another full run). When the task is `resolved`, run ONE fresh pristine cycle to confirm the signature clears; if it recurs after resolve or the task is `failed`/`denied`, ESCALATE. This is the "file it automatically, confirm filed→in-progress→resolved" contract (step 7 / step 7b).

DRIVE VIA CLI + RAW TMUX, NEVER THE tmux-cli MCP TOOLS. MCP tools are frozen into a Claude at startup; a mid-loop `make install` would not refresh them, so the orchestrator must use the `tmux-cli` CLI + `tmux send-keys`/`paste-buffer`/`load-buffer`/`pipe-pane` (re-read from disk every call) to survive target rebuilds without restarting (design §3).

TWO-STEP SEND, ALWAYS. Every DRIVE is text FIRST (`paste-buffer`/`send-keys -l "<text>"`, or `tmux load-buffer` + `paste-buffer` for multi-line/quoted input — no shell-escaping) THEN a SEPARATE `Enter` key. NEVER a combined send; the PoC failure mode was a forgotten/merged Enter (design §4).

CONFIG-THEN-RUN — SEED `~/.claude.json` TRUST BEFORE ANY CLAUDE LAUNCH. Because `tmux-cli start` auto-launches `claude`, pre-seed the target dir's `hasTrustDialogAccepted` + global `bypassPermissionsModeAccepted` in `~/.claude.json` BEFORE `start` — never cancel-then-reseed a trust dialog after the fact (step 2). The order is config, THEN run.

AUTONOMOUS KICKOFF + STAY IDLE — DRIVE ONCE, THEN REACT. After the handshake the channel is live, so the orchestrator's DRIVE is ONE self-contained AUTONOMOUS kickoff prompt: hand the target the product spec; it self-invokes `/tmux:project-discovery` and self-answers discovery NON-INTERACTIVELY from that spec, then self-drives roadmap → preflight-pause → taskvisor → app, emitting `notify-orchestrator` milestones. The orchestrator then STAYS IDLE and reacts to those milestones — it does NOT `capture-pane`/poll to gate sends or to answer interactive questions. The slash-command-then-prompt discipline (a bare `/command` must load before its prompt; a bundled `/command + prompt` in one submission is a drive defect) is honored INSIDE the target's own self-invocation, NOT by an orchestrator poll. Later milestone-gated interventions (e.g. `taskvisor-start` after the preflight gate) are each a single two-step send fired ON a milestone, never on a capture-poll (step 4).

INIT-PROMPT HANDSHAKE FIRST — ESTABLISH + TEST `notify-orchestrator` BEFORE ANY FLOW DRIVE. The orchestrator's VERY FIRST message to the target is an INIT PROMPT (a normal two-step send into the idle `❯`, sent BEFORE any `/command`) that (a) tells the target it runs UNDER the e2e-evaluator orchestrator, (b) makes `tmux-cli notify-orchestrator "<msg>"` its MANDATORY milestone-reporting contract for the whole run (the milestone vocabulary WATCH expects: discovery-done → roadmap-generated → preflight-passed → goals-dispatched → goals-done → app-up, plus `goal-<id> failed: <reason>`), and (c) orders an IMMEDIATE comms-test — the target runs `tmux-cli notify-orchestrator "E2E-HANDSHAKE-OK <session>"` right away. The orchestrator then VERIFIES that token actually landed in its OWN pane (`TMUX_CLI_ORCHESTRATOR_PANE`) BEFORE sending any flow slash-command. NO flow DRIVE happens until the handshake passes: a dead primary channel would silently degrade WATCH to blind polling — the exact defect this guards (step 3b).

NEVER `pkill -f <projectname>` IN TEARDOWN — it matches the killer's OWN command line and SIGTERMs the teardown mid-run (observed: exit 144, `rm` skipped). Reap by EXACT session name + tracked PIDs only (design §10).

PIPE-PANE BEFORE ANY DRIVE. Start the pipe-pane log in BOOTSTRAP the instant the pane exists and before the first DRIVE, with `-o` (append) so a re-attach never truncates — else early transcript is lost and assertions miss it (design §4).

PRISTINE EVERY CYCLE. A failed cycle's target session AND its `/tmp` content are reaped via TEARDOWN (§10) before the next cycle; never reuse a dirty target dir (PROVISION fails loud on a non-empty dir).

ATTACH THE HUMAN VIEW WHEN A GUI EXISTS. In PROVISION, whenever `$DISPLAY` is set AND a native terminal binary exists, you MUST attach a native terminal to the target session and VERIFY a client landed (`tmux list-clients`) before BOOTSTRAP — headless is permitted ONLY when there is genuinely no GUI (no `$DISPLAY` / no native terminal). Silently running headless with a GUI present hides progress from the operator and is a defect, not a shortcut (step 2).

COMPOSE, DON'T REIMPLEMENT. The orchestrator drives the REAL discovery/generation/daemon flow inside the target; it never re-derives discovery questions, goal enumeration, or the dispatch loop. Only a genuine tmux-cli defect (reproduces on a clean retry, §7) earns a `/tmux:task-report`; LLM variance and bad-input are retried/fixed, never filed.

SLOT DISCIPLINE. This slice authors ONLY steps 1, 1b, 2, 3, 3b, 11 (3b is BOOTSTRAP's comms-handshake tail). Steps 4–10 are labelled SLOT comments owned by execute-3 (loop) and execute-4 (state/report); do NOT author or renumber them.

step.title: CLI precondition
step.action: Assert the `tmux-cli` CLI is reachable on PATH (e.g. `command -v tmux-cli` / `tmux-cli --version` exits 0) and that `tmux`, `docker`, and `claude` are present.

check.condition: tmux-cli CLI not reachable on PATH
check.action: Print exactly: ERROR: tmux-cli CLI required on PATH but not found — install it (make install) and retry.

step.note: The tmux-cli MCP server is intentionally NOT a precondition — driving is CLI-only by design (§3). MCP-not-required-to-drive is a property, not a gap.

step.title: Read project context + resume state
step.action: Read AGENTS.md (or CLAUDE.md if AGENTS.md is absent) from THIS repo root and internalize conventions, architecture, and constraints before driving any cycle. If neither exists, proceed without project context.

startup-note.title: No prerequisite gate — reporting is task-report + lifecycle monitor (self-contained)
startup-note.rule: There is NO consumer-pipeline / auto-install-watcher prerequisite and NO soft-pause gate to PROBE FOR AT STARTUP. Defect reporting is `/tmux:task-report` filed autonomously by the conductor (honoring api.enabled / DEDUP / RECURSION-guard) — it needs no external consumer or rebuild watcher to file. After filing, the conductor MONITORS the filed task through `new` → `in_progress` → `resolved` (step 7b) and only re-tests a resolved fix; it does NOT re-cycle into an unresolved deterministic defect. So the loop does not BLIND-block on a fix, but it also does not THRASH: it monitors the filed task's lifecycle. Proceed straight to RESUME below — do not probe for, assert, or pause on any reporting prerequisite at startup.

resume.title: RESUME — fresh-from-scratch by DEFAULT; the state clear/init/resume is performed by e2e-bootstrap (step 2), the LLM only chooses the flag (the §6 resume read paired with step 10's write)
resume.rule: FRESH-FROM-SCRATCH IS THE DEFAULT — NEVER silently continue prior work. e2e-evaluator validates the flow end-to-end FROM SCRATCH on every invocation. Resuming is OPT-IN: pass `--resume` to `tmux-cli e2e-bootstrap` ONLY when $ARGUMENTS STRICTLY defines resume (a bare `resume` token or a `--resume` flag). Otherwise pass NO resume flag — a found prior STATE_FILE is WIPED, not resumed: e2e-bootstrap CLEARS the scenario's STATE_FILE + its `e2e-report-<scenario>-cycle-*.md` reports (plus any pre-scoping `e2e-report-cycle-*.md` legacy orphans — a one-time migration; another scenario's scoped reports are never swept), reaps stale `tmux-cli-tmp-*` sessions, and writes a fresh `"cycle": 1` in-progress state atomically. The LLM does NOT hand-edit the STATE_FILE here.
resume.action: Choose the flag from $ARGUMENTS; e2e-bootstrap (step 2) then implements the §6 read→reconcile→atomic-rewrite so you never hand-drive it. Its branch semantics: a MISSING state file is a FRESH run, NOT an error (Missing file = cycle 1, never a failure); status==`in-progress` with `cycle` <= `max_cycles` CONTINUES at `cycle` — because `cycle` is the NEXT cycle to run, cycles 1..`cycle`-1 are already done and are NOT re-run; an already-terminal status (passed | exhausted | escalated) STOPS cleanly; `cycle` > `max_cycles` sets status:exhausted and ESCALATEs (the budget is spent). The returned JSON's `cycle` is the cycle now running; execute-4's step-8/step-10 writes own the per-cycle bumps thereafter.

A resume may have been REACHED BY the step-7b managed session restart: the relaunched session's kickoff points at `.tmux-cli/e2e-evaluator/<scenario>.state.md` (the ledger's human/kickoff rendering), whose "Next action" line says to invoke `/tmux:e2e-evaluator resume`. When the resumed e2e-bootstrap JSON carries `verify_signature`/`verify_task_id`, this is a fix-verification resume — run ONE pristine cycle and have JUDGE confirm that signature cleared (step 7b action 4); do NOT treat it as an ordinary continue.

step.title: PROVISION + BOOTSTRAP + HANDSHAKE — one command: `tmux-cli e2e-bootstrap`
step.action: PARSE THE ONE JSON LINE e2e-bootstrap prints on stdout (progress goes to stderr). On `ok:true` capture `{session, target_pane, orchestrator_pane, target_dir, log_path, state_file, cycle, max_cycles, human_view}` and proceed to DRIVE (step 4) using those handles — the channel is proven live, NO further setup is needed. If `human_view` is a `headless (warn: …)` value while a GUI was expected, surface that WARN to the operator (a missing human view is a reportable harness defect, not a silent fallback).

check.action: It has ALREADY self-torn-down the partial target (kill-session + `rm -rf`, unless `--keep-on-failure`). Read `{stage, error}`: a `precondition`/`provision`/`start`/`pipe-pane` stage is a harness/infra defect (report via <error-reporting>); a `handshake` stage means the `notify-orchestrator` WATCH channel never proved live — do NOT proceed to DRIVE on a dead channel (the exact degrade this gate prevents). Abort the cycle; the next cycle re-provisions fresh.

step.title: BOOTSTRAP — folded into step 2's e2e-bootstrap (orchestrator-pane inject + pipe-pane)
step.action: BOOTSTRAP is performed by `tmux-cli e2e-bootstrap` (step 2): it exports `TMUX_CLI_ORCHESTRATOR_PANE` into the target launch env (the target's `tmux-cli notify-orchestrator "<msg>"` execute-1 primitive reads it to reply to this pane), launches claude with trust pre-accepted + bypass on, and starts the `pipe-pane` `-o` append log BEFORE any DRIVE (the transcript of record — assertions grep the log, not the screen). There is NO separate hand-driven BOOTSTRAP step: read the handles from step 2's JSON (`orchestrator_pane`, `target_pane`, `log_path`) and continue.

step.title: HANDSHAKE — folded into step 2's e2e-bootstrap (notify-orchestrator proven live)
step.action: The init-prompt HANDSHAKE is performed and VERIFIED by `tmux-cli e2e-bootstrap` (step 2): it sends the init prompt (two-step: `load-buffer`+`paste-buffer` THEN a separate Enter) that makes `tmux-cli notify-orchestrator` the target's mandatory milestone channel (vocabulary `discovery-done → roadmap-generated → preflight-passed → goals-dispatched → goals-done → app-up`, plus `goal-<id> failed: <reason>`) and orders the comms-test, then PROVES the channel live by finding `E2E-HANDSHAKE-OK <session>` + the notify success line in the pipe-pane log (retrying the prompt once before aborting). `ok:true` from step 2 MEANS the handshake passed — proceed straight to DRIVE (step 4); a `handshake`-stage `ok:false` already aborted+reaped the cycle. From here WATCH (step 5) idles on the now-proven `notify-orchestrator` channel — it does NOT poll the live channel.

step.title: DRIVE — self-healing loop body + two-step send
step.rule: SELF-HEALING LOOP CONTROL. Steps 4–7 are the cycle body; one CYCLE = PROVISION (step 2) → BOOTSTRAP (step 3) → DRIVE (4) → WATCH (5) → MEASURE (6) → JUDGE/TRIAGE (7) → REPORT (execute-4 steps 8–9) → TEARDOWN (step 11). Run AT MOST 10 cycles, PRISTINE each (TEARDOWN reaps the prior target before the next PROVISION). Maintain a cycle counter in run-state (execute-4's state.json).

check.condition: the cycle counter would exceed 10 (an 11th cycle)
check.action: Do NOT start another cycle. Set `status: exhausted` in run-state and ESCALATE to the human (the self-heal budget is spent — repeated failures need a person). STOP the loop. This is the loop guard; a max-10-cycle run never silently spins.

step.action: STAY IDLE — NEVER `capture-pane`/sleep-poll TO GATE A SEND (the operator-corrected time-waste). Once the handshake passes (step 3b) the channel is LIVE, so readiness is EVENT-DRIVEN, never polled: the target is known idle-and-awaiting-input at exactly two moments — (a) right after the handshake (proven idle by step 3b) and (b) the instant a `notify-orchestrator` milestone arrives (a milestone MEANS the target finished that phase and is now idle for the next instruction). Send ONLY at those moments, then return to idle (WATCH, step 5). Do NOT `capture-pane`/grep the pipe-pane log to "check if it loaded / is ready" before a send — that is precisely the live-channel polling the EVENT-DRIVEN WATCH mandate forbids. `capture-pane` is FALLBACK-ONLY (step 5: 600s (10 min) silence or a §9 ceiling breach).

TWO-STEP SEND, ALWAYS. Send the scenario instruction (and every later input) in TWO separate tmux calls: (1) the TEXT via `tmux load-buffer` + `tmux paste-buffer -t <target-pane>` (multi-line/quoted input — no shell-escaping), or `tmux send-keys -l` for a short single line; THEN (2) a SEPARATE `tmux send-keys -t <target-pane> Enter`. NEVER merge the text and the Enter — the PoC failure mode was a forgotten/merged Enter (design §4).

KICKOFF AS ONE AUTONOMOUS SELF-DRIVING PROMPT — so the orchestrator never polls between a bare `/command` and its prompt, and never has to answer interactive questions. The orchestrator's post-handshake DRIVE is ONE self-contained instruction (a single two-step send into the handshake-idle `❯`) that hands the target the full product SPEC and tells it to autonomously run the REAL flow NON-INTERACTIVELY: the target invokes `/tmux:project-discovery` ITSELF, answers discovery FROM THE SUPPLIED SPEC (it must NOT stop to ask the orchestrator questions), then drives roadmap-generation → (PAUSE and notify at the preflight gate, step 4b) → taskvisor → running app, emitting each `notify-orchestrator` milestone as it lands. Because the TARGET (not the orchestrator) types the slash command, the slash-command-then-prompt discipline is honored INSIDE the target (it sends the bare `/command` as its own submission, lets it load, then feeds its own prompt) — the orchestrator never issues a bare `/command` + separate prompt itself, so it never needs a capture-poll to detect "loaded". A bundled `/command + prompt` in ONE submission is still a drive defect. After the kickoff lands the orchestrator IDLES (step 5) and sends again ONLY when a milestone gates the next intervention (e.g. roadmap-generated → preflight (step 4b) → drive `taskvisor-start`), each as its own milestone-gated two-step send — never a polled one. Drive the REAL flow; never re-derive discovery questions or the goal enumeration.

action.title: REQUIRE HEARTBEATS through the long silent phases

step.action: The kickoff prompt MUST also instruct the target to emit INTERMEDIATE HEARTBEAT milestones through the two phases that legitimately run 10+ min in one silent LLM turn, so WATCH stays event-driven and does not fall back to blind capture-pane polling: `tmux-cli notify-orchestrator "discovery-progress <note>"` at each discovery section it completes, and `tmux-cli notify-orchestrator "roadmap-progress <n>/<total>"` after each bounded-context/goal skeleton it authors. These are heartbeats, not phase transitions (step 5 resets the silence timer on each without advancing the milestone cursor). Without them the roadmap phase alone silently burns the whole 600s window every run (observed) — a healthy phase should never be indistinguishable from a hang.
After the instruction lands, hand control to WATCH (step 5) — do NOT poll the live channel; the target reports milestones via `tmux-cli notify-orchestrator`.

step.title: Preflight — HARD GATE between roadmap-generated and taskvisor-start
step.rule: This gate runs ONCE per cycle, AFTER the target's roadmap/`goals.yaml` is generated and elaborated, and BEFORE the target is driven to `taskvisor-start`. Real parallelism needs ALL of: the cap flipped, a parallelizable DAG, disjoint scopes, and host capacity (design §13/§14.1). A FAILED preflight BLOCKS `taskvisor-start` for this cycle (do not DRIVE the start) — the roadmap must be corrected (a planner/elaborator defect, not a worker one) before goals are consumed.

EARLY-DISPATCH PIPELINE — THE GATE MAY ARRIVE AFTER goal-001 IS ALREADY RUNNING. `task-plan-generate` with `execution: dispatch` now PIPELINES: it dispatches goal-001 (the env gate) and starts the daemon BEFORE the whole roadmap is generated (commit 8eece21), so the `roadmap-generated` milestone can co-arrive with (or trail) a `goals-dispatched` where goal-001 is already `running`. Do NOT treat that as a contract violation and do NOT block/kill the already-running gate goal. Instead: (a) the cap override (action 1) is a LIVE file re-read every tick, so setting it now still governs every subsequent slot-fill — apply it regardless; (b) run the DAG + scope audit (actions 2–3) against the elaborated `goals.yaml` all the same; (c) if the audit FAILS, the gate can no longer "not DRIVE start" (start already happened) — so route the over-serialization as a planner/elaborator defect via TRIAGE (step 7) AND record it as a MEASURE perf signal, rather than pretending it blocked. Blocking-before-start remains correct ONLY when the roadmap is generated fully before any dispatch.

action.title: Set the cap (runtime override, no restart)

step.action: Write `max_goals=3` via the `.tmux-cli/taskvisor-concurrency` runtime override (`ConcurrencyOverridePath()`, concurrency.go:28) — NOT `setting.yaml` (which would need a daemon restart). N=3 is the conservative start for one dev host; step 5 below may raise it to 4 ONLY after host capacity is confirmed.
Run plan-audit over the generated `goals.yaml` using `DetectOverSerialized` (depinfer.go:329) — do NOT hand-roll a DAG check.

check.condition: DetectOverSerialized returns a non-nil SerializationFinding (near-linear / fan-out≤1 / runnable≤1)
check.action: FAIL preflight and BLOCK `taskvisor-start`. The roadmap is over-serialized (no concurrency possible); the produce/consume `depends_on` must be fixed before start. Route as a planner/elaborator defect via TRIAGE (step 7), not a worker retry.

step.action: Assert the about-to-run-concurrently goals declare disjoint file `Scope` using `DisjointReadySet(maxGoals)` (scope_gate.go:162) — the same gate the scheduler uses in slot-fill (statemachine.go:160). NOTE: `task-plan-generate` output commonly carries NO `scope` field at all — only a coarse `deliverable_area` (e.g. `projects/api/src/Http/Controller/`). Absent `scope` is UNKNOWN scope → `DisjointReadySet` silently serializes; and when several ready goals share the same `deliverable_area` (observed: the three GET-action goals all carrying `projects/api/src/Http/Controller/`) they overlap even if scopes existed. Check BOTH: missing `scope`, and shared `deliverable_area` across the ready fan-out.

check.condition: DisjointReadySet would serialize the ready set (overlapping OR unknown scope), OR the ready fan-out shares a `deliverable_area`
check.action: FAIL preflight, FLAGGING the offending goal(s) whose Scope overlaps/is unknown or whose `deliverable_area` collides (unknown scope = silently serialized → achieved parallelism collapses to ~1 despite `max_goals=3`). Route as a planner/elaborator over-serialization defect via TRIAGE (step 7); if start has NOT happened yet, also BLOCK `taskvisor-start` until the roadmap emits disjoint scopes.

step.action: Confirm goals are sized to ~20 min of work each (the 20-min/goal target is achieved by SIZING + parallelism, not by a bigger ceiling — design §14.3). Oversized goals are a roadmap-correction signal, not a ceiling bump.

Verify the host can run N concurrent docker compose stacks + worktrees (CPU/RAM) with NO host-port collisions (stacks are network-internal by default per the stack convention). Only if capacity is confirmed may N be raised to 4; otherwise keep 3.

action.title: Set the ceilings as soft guardrails

step.action: Set `max_wall_clock_sec` ∈ [1800, 2700] (a generous 30–45 min safety net) — NEVER a tight 30-min-minus value that would kill legitimately-borderline goals; enforced softly. Keep `progress_timeout_sec` ≈ 300 (config.go:93) so a wedged goal dies in ~5 min instead of eating the whole ceiling — this is the DAEMON's goal-kill cadence and is INDEPENDENT of the orchestrator's WATCH silence window (step 5), which is the longer 600s (10 min) so long, legitimately-silent pre-daemon LLM phases (discovery, roadmap elaboration) don't false-trigger a fallback.

step.title: WATCH — event-driven primary, capture-pane fallback on silence
step.rule: EVENT-DRIVEN, NEVER POLL A LIVE CHANNEL. The PRIMARY signal is the target reporting milestones via `tmux-cli notify-orchestrator "<msg>"` (execute-1 primitive) into THIS orchestrator's pane (`TMUX_CLI_ORCHESTRATOR_PANE`, injected at BOOTSTRAP). Idle and react to those reports — do not `sleep`-poll or screen-scrape while the channel is live (design §4).

branch.action: Record the event, RESET the silence timer, and advance WATCH to the next expected milestone (discovery-done → roadmap-generated → preflight-passed → goals-dispatched → goals-done → app-up). The reset is what keeps a healthy long-running phase from tripping the fallback.

action.title: HEARTBEATS keep long silent phases event-driven

branch.action: The two longest phases (discovery, roadmap generation+elaboration) legitimately run 10+ min in a single LLM turn, so they routinely trip the blind 600s fallback even when perfectly healthy. To keep WATCH event-driven instead of fallback-driven, the kickoff contract (step 4) requires the target to emit INTERMEDIATE HEARTBEAT milestones through those phases — `discovery-progress <note>` and `roadmap-progress <n>/<total>` (one per bounded-context/goal skeleton authored). A heartbeat is a normal milestone: record it, RESET the silence timer, keep idling — it is NOT a phase transition and does NOT advance the expected-milestone cursor. A phase that goes 600s WITHOUT even a heartbeat is a stronger wedge signal than one from a phase that never promised heartbeats.

branch.tag: goal-<id> failed milestone arrives — PRIMARY wedge surface
branch.action: A `goal-<id> failed: <reason>` milestone is the PRIMARY, DETERMINISTIC wedge signal — do NOT wait out the 600s silence window for it. The daemon's poll-error fail-fast now ALWAYS surfaces a wedge as a real failure: on K (= `circuit_breaker_k`) identical poll/bring-up errors it marks the goal failed via the existing failure path and emits the `[TASKVISOR:ALL-COMPLETE …failed=N]` / `goal-<id> failed` milestone instead of looping forever. On this milestone, treat the cycle as a candidate FAIL and go STRAIGHT to JUDGE/TRIAGE (step 7) — the FAIL branch there AUTO-runs `tmux-cli e2e-teardown <session>`. The 600s-silence and §9 ceiling-breach triggers below remain the BACKSTOP only for a target that wedges WITHOUT surfacing a failure milestone (e.g. a hung pre-daemon LLM phase). A best-effort EXTRA corroboration, ONLY where the daemon window's own log is observable: `>=K` identical `poll error:` lines — but do NOT build the wedge detection to depend on it, the conductor pipe-pane captures the target Claude pane, not the daemon window.

timeout.rule: Fire the FALLBACK only on (a) 600s (10 min) of heartbeat-SILENCE (no `notify-orchestrator` line) OR (b) ANY phase breaching its §9 hard ceiling — whichever first. The 10-min window is deliberately LONGER than the daemon's `progress_timeout_sec` (~300, config.go:93): pre-daemon LLM phases (discovery, roadmap generation + elaboration) legitimately run silent for well over 5 min, so a 300s window false-triggers a fallback every few minutes (the operator-corrected too-fast tick). 600s is long enough to never false-trigger on a healthy phase yet still catch a genuine hang; the daemon independently kills a wedged goal-window at its own ~300s `progress_timeout_sec` cadence. The ceiling breach is the second, independent trigger (regardless of the silence timer).
timeout.action: FALLBACK DIAGNOSIS (read-only): `grep` the PIPE-PANE LOG and run `tmux capture-pane -p -t <target-pane>` to read the target's current state. If wedged (no progress, error, hung spinner) → hand to JUDGE/TRIAGE (step 7) as a candidate hang.

SLOW-BUT-HEALTHY IS NOT "JUST VARIANCE" — DIAGNOSE THE CAUSE, DON'T SILENTLY RESUME. When the fallback finds real progress (the channel merely went quiet), do NOT simply return to WATCH: first CLASSIFY WHY the phase is slow and RECORD it as a perf signal that feeds MEASURE (step 6) + the REPORT (step 9). Root-cause buckets: (1) MONOLITHIC-TURN — a long single LLM turn with no intermediate milestone (e.g. roadmap generation authored in one pass); (2) OVER-DECOMPOSITION — far more goals than the app warrants, inflating both roadmap-gen time and the serial critical path; (3) MISSING/OVERLAPPING SCOPE — ready goals share (or omit) file scope / `deliverable_area`, so `DisjointReadySet` serializes them and achieved parallelism collapses to ~1 despite `max_goals>1`; (4) HOST-LOAD — CPU/RAM saturation. Buckets (2)+(3) are STRUCTURAL (planner/elaborator over-serialization): route them to TRIAGE (step 7) as a planner/elaborator over-serialization defect signal, NOT a worker retry — do not just wait them out. Only bucket (4) and genuine one-off model noise are treated as variance (resume WATCH). Every slow-but-healthy fallback appends a one-line `slow: <phase> cause=<bucket>` note to the cycle's perf signals.

step.action: Continue WATCHing until the cycle reaches a terminal milestone (app-up = candidate PASS) or a fallback diagnoses a wedge (candidate FAIL) — then proceed to MEASURE (step 6) and JUDGE/TRIAGE (step 7).

step.title: MEASURE — per-phase timings + achieved parallelism (soft guardrails)
step.action: Record start/end timestamps for EVERY goal and EVERY phase (`elaborate`, `implement`, `investigate`, `validate`) — taskvisor already drives these transitions; read them from goal state + the pipe-pane log, do not re-instrument the daemon (design §9).

Across cycles, compute per-phase p50 / p90 / p95. Set the WARN threshold at observed p90 and the hard ceiling at `max(absolute §9 ceiling, p95 × 1.5)`. Flag any goal/phase over WARN (investigate) or over the ceiling (treat as a hang → fallback diagnosis (step 5) → TRIAGE (step 7)).

Record the MEAN in-flight goals per tick (achieved parallelism) vs `max_goals`. Achieved-parallelism ≪ N at `max_goals>1` is ITSELF a defect signal — the DAG/scopes serialized; route it as a planner/elaborator defect (over-serialized `depends_on` or non-disjoint Scope), NOT a worker retry (design §14.3).
step.rule: Timing thresholds are SOFT guardrails tuned from observed data — they feed reports + defect signals, they do not hard-kill goals (the daemon's `max_wall_clock_sec`/`progress_timeout_sec` own enforcement).

step.title: JUDGE + TRIAGE — verify end-state, file defects only on reproduce
step.action: JUDGE against the §8 end-state (full-flow scenario): discovery produced `docs/architecture/*`; a roadmap `goals.yaml` was generated; taskvisor drove all goals to `done`; AND the app is actually up — `GET /login` → 200, dashboard route unauthenticated → 302/401, authenticated request → 200 (a green daemon with a dead app is a FALSE pass). Combine with the MEASURE verdict: GREEN requires the build passing AND p90 within target (design §8/§13).

branch.action: Stop the loop: hand to execute-4's SUCCESS step (step 10). No TRIAGE, no task-report.

branch.tag: FAIL — end-state unmet or a phase wedged/over-ceiling
branch.action: AUTO-TEARDOWN ON WEDGE — NO LLM DELIBERATION. The instant a wedge is detected (a `goal-<id> failed` milestone from the daemon poll-error fail-fast, OR a fallback-diagnosed hang / §9 ceiling breach), AUTO-run `tmux-cli e2e-teardown <session>` (step 11's exact reap, on the EXACT session name from step 2's JSON) and record the cycle as FAIL in the state ledger (step 8) DETERMINISTICALLY — do NOT wait on a judge or deliberate. This is the no-operator-intervention reap that satisfies AC4/AC5: the wedged session, its worktrees, and the disposable `/tmp/<scenario>-<ts>` dir are reclaimed automatically. The defect-vs-variance-vs-bad-input classification below governs ONLY the reporting decision (whether to file a `/tmux:task-report`); it NEVER gates this teardown.
Enter TRIAGE. Classify the failure with the defect-vs-variance-vs-bad-input rule below; only a tmux-cli DEFECT earns a `/tmux:task-report` (design §7).

step.rule: AUTO-FILE — NEVER ASK THE OPERATOR WHETHER TO REPORT. Filing a confirmed tmux-cli defect is AUTOMATIC and autonomous: the instant TRIAGE classifies a failure as a tmux-cli DEFECT, the orchestrator files `/tmux:task-report` ITSELF — it does NOT pause, present options, or use AskUserQuestion to get permission to file (asking-to-file is the operator-corrected miss). The operator can always inspect the filed task afterwards.

table.title: Failure triage (decision authority: orchestrator; a defect is CONFIRMED by EITHER a clean-retry reproduce OR code-confirmed determinism)
table.row: the failure is in the TOOLING (dispatch / worktree / compose / elaboration / daemon / planner-over-serialization), not the worker's code choices, AND is confirmed by EITHER (a) reproducing on a clean PRISTINE retry with the SAME DEFECT SIGNATURE, OR (b) CODE-CONFIRMED DETERMINISM — inspection of the tmux-cli source shows the failing path is an unconditional/deterministic code behavior (e.g. an ungated hard-fail), which is STRONGER than one runtime reproduce and does NOT require burning a second full cycle to re-observe → AUTO-FILE `/tmux:task-report` IMMEDIATELY. Prefer code-confirmation when a reproduce cycle is expensive.
NOT code-deterministic AND does NOT reproduce on a clean retry (a one-off bad worker choice / model noise) → RECORD as variance, retry the cycle, NEVER file a task-report.
the scenario/instruction itself was malformed → FIX the input, retry, NEVER file a task-report.

step.rule: DEFECT SIGNATURE = the normalized {phase, reason-class, area} tuple — lowercase and STRIP volatile goal-instance ids/timestamps (goal-007 vs goal-008, run stamps) so the SAME defect dedupes across cycles. This signature powers both DEDUP and the loop guard below.
step.action: On a confirmed defect (clean-retry reproduce OR code-confirmed determinism): AUTO-invoke the `/tmux:task-report` skill (DEDUP key = category+title+goal_id; `proposed_fix` non-stub; honoring api.enabled / RECURSION-guard) — do NOT re-implement reporting here (see task-report.xml). Capture the returned `{id, status}`, write it into the cycle's `history[]` (`task_id`, `task_status`) and the REPORT_FILE's Filed Task section, then TEARDOWN (step 11). The orchestrator NEVER edits/pulls/rebuilds the tooling itself — it FILES and then MONITORS the fix landing (step 7b).

step-ref.title: DEFECT LIFECYCLE MONITOR — confirm filed → in_progress → resolved before re-testing
step-ref.rule: After AUTO-filing, the orchestrator MONITORS the filed task through its backend lifecycle instead of immediately burning a fresh cycle against the still-unfixed signature (re-cycling into an unresolved deterministic defect is thrash — it just re-hits the same wedge for ~another full run). Poll `tmux-cli` backend task state via the `task-list` MCP tool (filter by this machine's `fingerprint`, find the filed `id`) — this is the BACKEND task ledger, NOT the live target channel, so polling it is allowed (the EVENT-DRIVEN-WATCH no-poll rule governs only the live target pane). Pace the poll SLOWLY (a human/agent must edit + land the fix): ~20–30 min between checks via a background-sleep watchdog that re-invokes the conductor; announce each transition (`new` → `claimed`/`in_progress` → `resolved`|`failed`). The monitor is the mechanism that satisfies "confirm the defect was filed, is in progress, and resolved".

branch.tag: task resolved — SELF-UPDATE HANDOFF: managed session restart onto the fresh binary, then ONE verification cycle
branch.action: RECORD THE PENDING VERIFICATION (machine-written): run `tmux-cli e2e-state record --scenario <slug> --outcome failed --signature "<sig>" --task-id <id> --task-status resolved --app-up false [--git-after <sha>] --verify-signature "<sig>" --verify-task-id <id>`. The paired verify flags set the ledger's `verify` field ("the NEXT cycle is a fix-verification cycle for this signature/task"); every e2e-state write also renders the human/kickoff-readable `.tmux-cli/e2e-evaluator/<scenario>.state.md` alongside the JSON (same atomic temp+rename) — the handoff artifact the relaunched session reads.

RESTART-LOOP GUARD: run `tmux-cli e2e-state mark-self-update --scenario <slug> --task-id <id> --at <RFC3339 now>`. `ok:true` stamps `last_self_update` and licenses ONE managed session restart for this resolved task. `ok:false` (a restart was already performed for this task-id, or the run is terminal) → SKIP the restart and go straight to the verification cycle (action 4) — never restart twice for one resolved task; that restart loop is exactly what this guard exists to prevent.

MANAGED SESSION RESTART onto the fresh binary: run `tmux-cli self-update --restart session --resume-state .tmux-cli/e2e-evaluator/<scenario>.state.md` and parse its ONE JSON line. On `binary_changed:false` the reinstall was a no-op (binary already current) — no restart happened or was needed; fall through to the verification cycle (action 4) on the current binary. On a real session restart THIS conductor ends here: the relaunch (`start-attach --resume-state`) points the new supervisor at state.md, whose "Next action" line says to invoke `/tmux:e2e-evaluator resume` — the verification continues there with the MCP/command schemas re-frozen against the new binary instead of re-testing the stale one (the false-escalation hole this handoff closes). The rebuild is command-managed; the conductor still never hand-builds or `git pull`s.

VERIFICATION CYCLE: `tmux-cli e2e-bootstrap <scenario> --resume` surfaces the pending check as `verify_signature`/`verify_task_id` in its JSON — run ONE fresh PRISTINE cycle and have JUDGE (step 7) confirm that the DEFECT SIGNATURE cleared (or the run reaches app-up). Cleared → the self-heal converged: record the cycle with a plain `e2e-state record` WITHOUT verify flags (omitting them clears the ledger's `verify` field). Recurs after the resolve → ESCALATE per the branch below. The CLI is re-read from disk every call, so the re-test naturally exercises the binary the fix installed.

branch.tag: task failed/denied, or same signature recurs AFTER a resolve
branch.action: ESCALATE to the human and STOP — do NOT re-file (already filed + deduped) and do NOT keep re-cycling. A defect that was marked resolved yet still reproduces, or a task the backend `failed`/`denied`, needs a person. Same-signature-recurrence-after-resolve is the convergence loop guard (design §7).

branch.tag: monitor budget/timeout — task still unresolved after a long wait
branch.action: If the task lingers `new`/`in_progress` beyond a reasonable monitor budget (operator-tunable; default several hours of slow polling), record the run as PAUSED-AWAITING-FIX in the state ledger and surface a concise status to the operator — do NOT thrash cycles against the unfixed defect meanwhile.

check.condition: the SAME defect signature recurs across cycles (already filed once, still failing) BEFORE a resolve
check.action: Do NOT re-file (already filed + deduped) and do NOT burn another cycle — fall back to the step-7b monitor (wait for the filed task to resolve), not a blind re-cycle.

step.title: STATE — record the finished cycle with `tmux-cli e2e-state record` (§6, deterministic writer)
step.rule: STATE_FILE = `.tmux-cli/e2e-evaluator/<scenario>.state.json` is the durable cycle ledger that the self-healing loop (step 4 — its `cycle` counter and `status: exhausted` write live HERE) and RESUME (step 1b) both read. Schema (§6): `{ "scenario", "cycle", "max_cycles": 10, "status", "history": [ {cycle, outcome, defect_signature, task_reported, task_status, git_after, app_up, durations} ] }`; `status` ∈ `in-progress` | `passed` | `exhausted` | `escalated`.

MACHINE-WRITTEN ONLY — NEVER hand-write or hand-edit the ledger JSON (LLM-authored JSON is the schema-drift risk this command removed). Record the finished cycle by RUNNING `tmux-cli e2e-state record --scenario <slug> --outcome failed|passed --signature "<phase/reason-class/area or none>" [--task-id <id>] [--task-status <status>] --app-up true|false [--git-after <sha>] [--durations-json '<json object>']`. Flag mapping: `--scenario` = the slug from step 2's JSON; `--outcome` = the JUDGE verdict (step 7); `--signature` = the normalized DEFECT SIGNATURE tuple or the literal `none`; `--task-id`/`--task-status` = the filed `/tmux:task-report` id + backend status (omit when none filed); `--app-up` = the step-10 probe boolean; `--git-after` = the target repo's HEAD sha after the cycle; `--durations-json` = MEASURE's per-phase durations object (step 6); `--verify-signature`/`--verify-task-id` = PAIRED, step-7b-only: flag the NEXT cycle as the fix-verification of a resolved task's defect signature — omitting them on a later record clears the pending verification. Every record also renders the `<scenario>.state.md` handoff alongside the JSON. It prints one JSON line `{ok, scenario, cycle, status}` on stdout (mirror of e2e-bootstrap's surface); `ok:false` + non-zero exit means the record was REFUSED — the command never initializes a missing ledger (init is e2e-bootstrap's job, step 1b/2), so a refusal means the prologue was skipped or the ledger is corrupt: fix that, do not hand-write the file.

ATOMIC WRITE ONLY — the command writes to a `<scenario>.state.json.tmp` temp file then `rename(2)`s over the target (temp + rename, last-writer-wins), modelled on task-list.xml's atomic ledger rewrite (:44-68). NEVER truncate-in-place: a concurrent re-invocation must never observe a half-written file (resume idempotency; TestState_AtomicRewrite).

`cycle` SEMANTICS (documentation of what the command does — you never perform the bump yourself) — `cycle` is the NEXT cycle to run, NOT the last completed. The command appends the `history[]` entry stamped with the just-finished cycle, THEN bumps. Run it ONLY AFTER the REPORT step (9) has written its report; the just-finished cycle's `history[]` entry carries `cycle = cycle-1` (post-bump). Never off-by-one.
step.action: On a FAILED cycle: `--outcome failed` appends the `history[]` entry (`cycle`, `outcome:"failed"`, `defect_signature`, `task_reported`, `task_status`, `git_after`, `app_up`, `durations` from MEASURE step 6) and bumps `cycle += 1`; keeps `status: "in-progress"`.

On the max-cycle guard (step 4 loop guard / RESUME): `--outcome failed` at the `max_cycles` boundary REFUSES the bump and sets `status: "exhausted"` instead (the exact value execute-3's loop guard and RESUME both expect) — `cycle` never passes `max_cycles`.
On a PASSED cycle (step 10 SUCCESS): `--outcome passed` sets `status: "passed"`, appends the final `history[]` entry (with `app_up:true`), and does NOT bump `cycle` — the loop stops.

step.title: REPORT — write the per-cycle markdown report, then append history + rewrite state (§6)
step.rule: MACHINE-WRITTEN FILE — NEVER hand-author or hand-edit REPORT_FILE = `.tmux-cli/e2e-evaluator/e2e-report-<scenario>-cycle-<n>.md` (LLM-hand-written naming/section-order/atomicity is the drift risk this command removed). Post-cycle (after JUDGE/TRIAGE, step 7), write it by RUNNING `tmux-cli e2e-state report --scenario <slug> --cycle <n> --driven-summary "…" --failure-point "…" --defect-signature "…" --filed-task "…" --timing-table "…" --verdict PASS|FAIL|EXHAUSTED --verdict-reason "…" --app-up true|false`. The section PROSE stays conductor-authored (each flag carries the matching section's content per the six section defs below); the naming (scenario-scoped, the slug from step 2's JSON), the EXACT FIXED section order, and the atomic temp+rename write are the command's. Flag mapping: `--scenario` = the slug from step 2's JSON; `--cycle` = the JUST-FINISHED cycle (the ledger's current `cycle` — record has NOT bumped it yet; the explicit cross-check is the safety feature); `--driven-summary`/`--failure-point`/`--defect-signature`/`--filed-task`/`--timing-table` = sections 1–5; `--verdict` = the JUDGE verdict enum; `--verdict-reason` = its one-line reason (rendered `<VERDICT> — <reason>`); `--app-up` = the step-10 probe boolean. Run it BEFORE step 8's `e2e-state record`: the ledger must still be `in-progress` at the just-finished cycle. It prints one JSON line `{ok, scenario, cycle, path}` (mirror of e2e-state record's surface) and NEVER mutates the ledger; `ok:false` + non-zero exit means the report was REFUSED (skipped prologue / missing ledger, wrong `--cycle`, terminal ledger, EXHAUSTED off the max_cycles boundary, or PASS with `--app-up false`) — fix the input, do NOT hand-write the file. Every section present every cycle:
step.section: what the cycle drove: scenario, target dir/session, and the phases reached (discovery → roadmap → preflight → taskvisor → app-up).
the phase + milestone where the cycle failed (or "none — passed"); for a wedge, the silence/§9-ceiling trigger that fired (step 5).
the normalized {phase, reason-class, area} tuple (step 7) with volatile goal-instance ids/timestamps stripped — or "none" on PASS / variance / bad-input.
the `/tmux:task-report` task id + its current `/tmux:task-list` status (e.g. `task-281` / `resolved`) — or "none filed (variance / bad-input / pass)".

per-phase p90 (elaborate / implement / investigate / validate) AND the mean in-flight goals (achieved parallelism), BOTH from execute-3's MEASURE (step 6, §9/§13). A p90 over target, or mean in-flight ≪ `max_goals`, is flagged here as a perf/over-serialization signal.
PASS | FAIL | EXHAUSTED, with the one-line reason. PASS REQUIRES `app_up:true` (step 10 false-pass guard) — never declare PASS on "all goals done" alone.
step.action: After the report command succeeds, run the step-8 `tmux-cli e2e-state record` command — it appends the cycle's `history[]` entry and rewrites STATE_FILE atomically; never hand-write either. Report-THEN-state ordering keeps the report on disk BEFORE the `cycle` bump points the next resume past it (no report-less gap on a mid-write crash).

step.title: SUCCESS-CRITERIA + green-stop — the §8 end-state JUDGE (step 7) consults; the app-up probe is the gate
step.rule: SUCCESS-CRITERIA (consulted by execute-3's JUDGE, step 7). FULL-FLOW PASS requires ALL FOUR, ANDed: (1) discovery docs present (`docs/architecture/*`); (2) a roadmap `goals.yaml` was generated; (3) taskvisor drove ALL goals to `done`; (4) the app is VERIFIABLY UP (the app-up probe below).
step.action: APP-UP PROBE (the gate): `docker compose up` the target stack, then assert `GET /login` → 200, the dashboard route UNAUTHENTICATED → 302/401, and an AUTHENTICATED request → 200. Record the boolean as `app_up` in the cycle's `history[]` entry (step 8).
step.rule: FALSE-PASS GUARD — JUDGE may NOT return PASS while `app_up:false`. "All goals done" alone is NEVER a pass: a green daemon with a dead app is a false pass (design §8 deliverable-pinning). If every goal is `done` but the app-up probe fails, the verdict is FAIL with `app_up:false` → route to TRIAGE (step 7). The app-up probe — not goal status — is the gate.
step.action: SINGLE-COMMAND secondary mode: when the thing-under-test is a single command (not the full flow), derive the intended end-state from THAT command's own skill/XML `description`/contract and assert the concrete artifacts it CLAIMS to produce (design §8 secondary).

branch.tag: PASS — all four criteria green AND app_up:true AND p90 within target (§13)
branch.action: Set `status: "passed"` (step 8), write the final report (step 9), and STOP the loop — no further cycles, no TRIAGE, no task-report.

branch.tag: not green — any criterion unmet, or app_up:false, or a phase over §9 ceiling
branch.action: Verdict FAIL → JUDGE hands to TRIAGE (step 7). If this was already the max cycle, the RESUME / step-4 loop guard sets `status: "exhausted"` and escalates instead of re-cycling.

step.title: TEARDOWN — one command: `tmux-cli e2e-teardown <session>` (subroutine the loop calls between cycles and at terminal stop)
step.action: RUN `tmux-cli e2e-teardown <session>` (the EXACT session name from step 2's JSON; pass `--dir <target_dir>` if the session is already gone and the dir must still be reaped). It performs the ordered, best-effort reap so you never hand-drive it: (1) stop the target's taskvisor daemon → (2) `docker compose down` EVERY stack the run created (network-internal by default, but still leak + collide on host ports if not downed) → (3) remove the run's git worktrees + their `taskvisor/<goal-id>` branches → (4) `tmux kill-session -t <target-session>` by EXACT recorded name → (5) `rm -rf` the disposable `/tmp/<scenario>-<ts>` dir (only dirs under `/tmp` are removed). A step's failure is LOGGED and the reap CONTINUES (mirrors the per-worktree compose-stack ordering: down stacks before removing the worktrees that hold them, design §10).
step.rule: e2e-teardown reaps by EXACT session name + the tracked dir ONLY — it NEVER `pkill -f <projectname>` (or any cmdline pattern that matches THIS teardown), which would SIGTERM the teardown itself (exit 144, `rm` skipped). Do NOT add a `pkill -f` step yourself (design §10).
step.note: Killing the target session also drops its attached human-view terminal — that is correct (the view is tied to the disposable target's lifecycle), not a defect.

execution-rules.rule: DRIVE VIA CLI + RAW TMUX, NEVER the tmux-cli MCP tools: `tmux-cli` CLI + `tmux send-keys`/`paste-buffer`/`load-buffer`/`pipe-pane`/`kill-session` are re-read from disk per call and survive a mid-loop `make install`; MCP tools are frozen at the target's Claude startup (design §3).
TWO-STEP SEND ALWAYS: text (`paste-buffer`/`send-keys -l`, or `load-buffer`+`paste-buffer` for multi-line/quoted) THEN a SEPARATE `Enter` — never combined (design §4).
NEVER `pkill -f <projectname>` in TEARDOWN — reap by exact session name + tracked PIDs only; a cmdline-pattern kill SIGTERMs the teardown (design §10).
PIPE-PANE in BOOTSTRAP before any DRIVE, `-o` (append); the log is the transcript of record — assertions grep the log, not the screen (design §4).
Self-test ONLY: never ship/point this at an end-user project; it drives disposable targets to verify + self-heal tmux-cli's own flow (design §14).
execution-rules.error-reporting: On a genuine harness/infra/spec defect (an MCP tool or `tmux-cli` command that errored after its own retries, a contradictory/under-specified goal spec, or a validator-error-class condition), follow the shared `error-reporting` procedure in `tmux/task-report.xml` — compose + submit autonomously, honoring api.enabled / DEDUP / RECURSION-guard. NEVER on expected work failures (red validate cycle, supervisor pushback, NEED_INPUT, unwritten-code test red) or daemon-owned goal-failures.

llm.critical-rules: • AUTOMATED PROLOGUE — run `tmux-cli e2e-bootstrap <scenario> [--project] [--resume]` for the WHOLE PROVISION/BOOTSTRAP/HANDSHAKE prologue (steps 1–3b) and `tmux-cli e2e-teardown <session>` for the ordered reap (step 11); parse e2e-bootstrap's one JSON line and proceed to DRIVE on `ok:true`. NEVER hand-drive those sub-steps with individual tmux/claude/JSON tool calls (the operator-corrected time-waste) • STATE LEDGER AND CYCLE REPORT ARE MACHINE-WRITTEN — write every per-cycle report with `tmux-cli e2e-state report …` (step 9) and record every finished cycle with `tmux-cli e2e-state record …` (step 8) AFTER the report is on disk; NEVER hand-author or hand-edit `<scenario>.state.json` or the REPORT_FILE (e2e-bootstrap initializes the ledger, e2e-state report writes the report, e2e-state record advances the ledger) • EVENT-DRIVEN WATCH — after the handshake, react to milestone messages as they arrive; NEVER capture-pane/sleep-poll a live channel. capture-pane/log-grep is FALLBACK-ONLY after 600s (10 min) silence or a §9 ceiling breach (step 5) • Drive the target via the tmux-cli CLI + raw tmux (send-keys/paste-buffer/load-buffer/pipe-pane) — NEVER the tmux-cli MCP tools (frozen at startup; CLI survives mid-loop rebuilds, design §3) • Two-step send always — text THEN a separate Enter; large/quoted input via load-buffer+paste-buffer (design §4) • Fresh-from-scratch by DEFAULT — never silently continue a prior run: CLEAR the scenario's STATE_FILE + its e2e-report-<scenario>-cycle-*.md reports (plus pre-scoping e2e-report-cycle-*.md legacy orphans, one-time migration) and reap stale `/tmp` test sessions from past runs (kill by EXACT `tmux-cli-tmp-*` name, never pkill -f) before starting cycle 1; resume is OPT-IN via an explicit `resume`/`--resume` arg (step 1b / step 2) • Config-then-run — seed ~/.claude.json trust (target hasTrustDialogAccepted + global bypassPermissionsModeAccepted) BEFORE tmux-cli start auto-launches claude; never fight the trust dialog after the fact (step 2) • Autonomous kickoff + STAY IDLE — DRIVE is ONE self-contained autonomous prompt (the target self-invokes /tmux:project-discovery and self-answers discovery NON-INTERACTIVELY from the supplied spec, then self-drives to app-up emitting milestones); then react to notify-orchestrator milestones and NEVER capture-pane/poll to gate a send or answer a question. The bare-/command-loads-before-its-prompt discipline is honored inside the target's own self-invocation; later interventions (e.g. taskvisor-start) are milestone-gated single two-step sends (step 4) • Init-prompt handshake FIRST — before ANY flow DRIVE, the orchestrator's first message establishes `tmux-cli notify-orchestrator` as the target's milestone-reporting contract AND tests it (target echoes `E2E-HANDSHAKE-OK <session>`, orchestrator verifies it landed in TMUX_CLI_ORCHESTRATOR_PANE); a dead channel aborts the cycle, never degrades silently to polling (step 3b) • TEARDOWN reaps in order (daemon → compose down → worktrees+branches → kill-session → rm -rf), continues past a single failure, and NEVER pkill -f <projectname> (design §10) • BOOTSTRAP injects TMUX_CLI_ORCHESTRATOR_PANE (the execute-1 notify-orchestrator contract) and starts pipe-pane BEFORE any DRIVE; PROVISION fails loud on a dirty dir (pristine every cycle) • PROVISION MUST attach a native terminal for the human view and VERIFY a client landed whenever $DISPLAY + a native terminal exist (Konsole first; exclude Flatpak) — headless ONLY with no GUI; never skip silently (step 2) • This slice authors ONLY steps 1/1b/2/3/3b/11 — steps 4–10 are execute-3 (loop) and execute-4 (state/report) SLOTs; do not author or renumber them • Self-test conductor only — never ship to drive end-user projects; only a defect that reproduces on a clean retry earns a task-report (design §7/§14)
